input.prompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

<current_datetime>2026-04-08T00:58:35.369Z</current_datetime>

I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,

input.initialPrompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

data.content:
Here's the standard approach:

### 1. Define each theme as a `ResourceDictionary`

Create three XAML files (e.g., `Themes/LightTheme.xaml`, `DarkTheme.xaml`, `HighContrastTheme.xaml`), each defining the same keys with different values:

```xml
<!-- Themes/LightTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
                    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
                    x:Class="MyApp.Themes.LightTheme">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#1A1A1A</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#0078D4</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

### 2. Merge a default theme in `App.xaml`

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <ResourceDictionary>
        <ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
            <themes:LightTheme />
        </ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
    </ResourceDictionary>
</Application.Resources>
```

### 3. Create a theme-switching service

```csharp
public static class ThemeService
{
    public static void ApplyTheme(string themeName)
    {
        var mergedDicts = Application.Current!.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
        mergedDicts.Clear();

        mergedDicts.Add(themeName switch
        {
            "Dark"         => new Themes.DarkTheme(),
            "HighContrast" => new Themes.HighContrastTheme(),
            _              => new Themes.LightTheme(),
        });

        // Persist the choice
        Preferences.Set("AppTheme", themeName);
    }
}
```

### 4. Wire it up on startup (`App.xaml.cs`)

```csharp
public App()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    ThemeService.ApplyTheme(Preferences.Get("AppTheme", "Light"));
}
```

### 5. Settings page picker

```xml
<Picker Title="Theme" SelectedIndexChanged="OnThemeChanged">
    <Picker.ItemsSource>
        <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}">
            <x:String>Light</x:String>
            <x:String>Dark</x:String>
            <x:String>HighContrast</x:String>
        </x:Array>
    </Picker.ItemsSource>
</Picker>
```

```csharp
void OnThemeChanged(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    if (sender is Picker p && p.SelectedItem is string theme)
        ThemeService.ApplyTheme(theme);
}
```

### Key points

- Use `{DynamicResource KeyName}` (not `StaticResource`) in all your XAML so controls react to the dictionary swap at runtime.
- Keep the same keys across all three dictionaries — the compiler won't catch mismatches, so a shared constants class or unit test helps.
- `Preferences` (from MAUI Essentials) persists the choice across launches.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to set up themed resource dictionaries in a .NET MAUI app with light, dark, and high-contrast themes, plus a settings page to switch between them at runtime. This is a general "how-to" question, not a code change in a repo. I'll give a concise architectural answer.
